F-A20-1.text: post-A9 marginal saving < 5pp on mixed JSON+text → MEASUREMENT
F-A20-1.measurement: Real-world corpora (nexus 4-file new + 6-file prior tick + anima cycle_log + airgenome rig_trend) all show 0% saving via gate REJECT. Synthetic high-repetition corpus shows 69%. A20 alone produces 0pp lift on aggregate post-A18 chain because real-world corpora are plain JSONL without `# schema:` headers — A20 has no schema-key surface to seed.
F-A20-1.raw_91_honest_c3: A20 design assumed HXC v1 headered corpora; production corpora are plain JSONL → A20 cannot fire. Pipelines that A20 helps require pre-conversion to HXC v1 wire format (which is itself a separable transform — not in this cycle's scope)

F-A20-2.measurement: 5/5 selftest fixtures (F2 JSON, F3 Korean, F4 ledger, F5 escape, F1 scaffold) byte-eq + 4/4 nexus small-file LIVE FIRE byte-eq round-trip = 9/9 round-trip CLEAR

F-A20-3.measurement: 0% on selftest F1.4 corpus-filter; A20 only emits forms present in ≥1 row body

F-A20-4.measurement: Memory: bounded by A20_VOCAB_SIZE=64 + per-row symbol arrays — CLEAR. Latency: encode of 19KB synthetic dense HXC v1 corpus exceeded 90s wall-clock under hexa_interp before manual termination → ≥4700ms/KB observed (94× threshold). hxc_a20 not yet AOT-compiled; the F-A20-4 latency floor is operationally relevant only when interpreter-bound. AOT path would likely re-clear F-A20-4 (analogous to F-A18-3 cliff dissolved by A18 AOT binary).
F-A20-4.raw_91_honest_c3: F-A20-4 latency falsifier FIRES under interpreter; expected to clear under AOT. Until A20 AOT binary built, A20 is not deployable in time-bounded sweep contexts (which is why prior tick 2 only used /tmp synthetic corpora and this LIVE FIRE limited to ≤30KB files)

F-A20-5.text: production-chain post-A12 placement standalone vs in-chain delta < 1pp (a18a1c7d new falsifier)
F-A20-5.measurement: A20 contributes 0pp standalone on real-world corpora → in-chain placement (post-A12) is moot. Cannot measure delta when the standalone signal is 0. Falsifier is non-discriminative this cycle; deferred to future cycle where A20 has a non-zero base.

retire_eligibility: NOT_ELIGIBLE_THIS_CYCLE — F-A20-1 (real-world saving) remains the active discriminator; falsifier should remain pre-registered until either (a) A20 AOT is built and a HXC-v1-converted corpus is sweep-tested, or (b) A20 retired in favour of future schema-aware variant

a20_chain_delta.standalone_a20_saving_on_real_corpora: 0% (gate REJECT on all measured real-world files)
a20_chain_delta.explanation: A20 eligibility gate (header_overhead vs amortised saving) suppresses encoding when no schema-key forms are corpus-present. Plain JSONL has no `# schema:` headers, so PASS 1 schema-key extraction yields empty pre-seed, and PASS 2 falls through to the gate. Net: A20 = identity passthrough on corpora measured.

target_80_verdict: NOT_YET_ACHIEVED — 4.58pp gap unchanged; A20 lift on current 6-repo corpus = 0pp

disclosures.note: A20 is correctness-preserving (raw 65+68 round-trip clear) but contributes 0pp to 6-repo aggregate because all measured corpora (nexus jsonl 95 files, anima 46 files, airgenome 2 files) are plain JSONL without `# schema:sN` headers. A20 PASS 1 (schema-key extraction) requires this header pattern. Without it, pre_seed is empty → vocab build is byte-pair only (equivalent to A9) → eligibility gate further REJECTs because per-schema overhead (vocab declarations + ^U sigils) exceeds savings. Therefore A20 ≈ identity for the entire 6-repo corpus as currently structured.

Prior anchor a6d36e1d (state/format_witness/2026-04-28_full_chain_6repo_aot_sweep.jsonl line 19) projected +3 to +6pp from A20 schema-aware BPE. This MEASUREMENT shows the projection assumed a HXC-v1-converted corpus that does not yet exist on the 6-repo file set. The +3 to +6pp would materialise if (a) corpora were first wrapped in HXC v1 with `# schema:sN` headers (separate transform), or (b) A20 PASS 1 were extended to derive schema keys from JSON top-level keys directly without the header. Neither is in this cycle's scope. raw 91 honest C3: prior projection was projection-labeled but the measurement gap is real.

F-A20-4 latency falsifier FIRES under hexa_interp (>4700ms/KB on 19KB dense synthetic corpus, 94× the 50ms/KB threshold). This is interpretive-cost dominated; AOT compilation (analogous to A18 AOT 7/7 selftest binary) would re-clear F-A20-4 (estimated <50ms/KB based on A18 AOT throughput precedent). A20 AOT binary build is on the rank-2 priority queue (A4-A15 AOT cohort 2 + A20 AOT).

All 9 round-trip tests (5 selftest + 4 nexus small-file LIVE FIRE) passed BYTE-EQ. raw 65+68 idempotent contract holds. raw 18 self-host fixpoint preserved (decoder = strict subset of encoder, both pure hexa). A20 is safe to leave LIVE in the algorithm catalog as a future-corpus enabler; it produces no harm on current corpora.

final_verdict: A20 PASS 3+4 fully LANDED at 1044 LoC (a45778db); selftest 8/8 PASS; round-trip byte-eq 9/9 CLEAR; F-A20-2/3 CLEAR. 6-repo byte-weighted aggregate post-A20 = 75.42% (UNCHANGED from a6d36e1d anchor; A20 contributes 0pp because real-world corpora lack HXC v1 headers). 80% TARGET = NOT_YET_ACHIEVED. Per-class: 2/4 ACHIEVED (text-heavy + structured-audit, regression-free), 2/4 BELOW (small-file 69.10%, mixed-real 63.75%; A20 cannot help these without corpus precondition). Honest C3: prior +3-6pp projection assumed precondition that current 6-repo state does not meet. Path forward = AOT cohort 2 (A4/A12/A15 structural binaries) + F-A18-1 PPMd tuning + A19 threshold tune; A20 remains LIVE for future HXC-v1-wrapped corpora and as a no-regression element in the algorithm catalog.
